mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the directional relationship reverses in 30%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 220 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) during periods of elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 8%) indicates resilience in demand, offsetting recession risks and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-UNRATE interaction fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the directional relationship reverses in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or the directional relationship reverses in 35%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with elevated consumer spending (RSAFS) signals robust demand for goods and services, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS interaction fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.04) or the directional relationship reverses in 25%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets that push wage growth and inflation, prompting monetary tightening and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant Fed rates (FEDFUNDS) signals policy inaction, increasing real interest rate uncertainty and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the directional relationship reverses in 30%+ of cases.
